content: tradedoubler.com 9 July 2020 GDPR FAQ This FAQ is made so that you can answer questions and fill in forms from advertisers and others (publishers, other colleagues) yourself. Please read this document carefully and check the questions before sending any GDPR related requests to Legal or DPO. Legal or DPO can’t reply to all GDPR related questions directly and some of the most frequent questions can be covered by the standard replies below. Question/Info Standard answer 1. What to do if advertiser sends me their own DPA and asks me to sign it? “Dear Advertiser, In order to become GDPR compliant we have developed a Tradedoubler standard Data Processing Agreement for Advertisers. This Personal Data Processing agreement (the “DPA”) is construed as an integral part of our signed Service Order. You can find our DPA on our website http://www.tradedoubler.com/en/gdpr- compliance/ . No re-approval or signature is required”. We do ask you to respect that we cannot manage or sign 2000 different versions of DPA´s with all our advertisers.

We thank you for your understanding.” If they still insist and if important to TD (high annual GP) send to Legal for review. 2. What if advertiser ask me what personal data Tradedoubler handles on their behalf as their data processor We process and store end consumer cookie id, IP number and advertiser´s order numbers and their contact details (name, email address and phone no) 3. What if advertiser ask me for how long does Tradedoubler store our personal data? Cookie id and IP numbers are automatically deleted 6 weeks after last transaction (of user journey), order numbers are stored according to local bookkeeping laws and local tax legislation. The reasons behind that are that order numbers are the basis for our invoicing and publisher payments, audit traceability.

content: tradedoubler.com 4. What if advertiser ask me do you rely on legitimate interests as the basis for processing Personal Data? Yes. To fulfil advertiser service agreement, contractual relationship, business relations 5. Can you logically separate the storage of the Personal Data of EU and non-EU residents? No 6. Can you logically separate the storage of the Personal Data provided by one advertiser from Personal Data provided by your other advertisers? Yes 7. Do you transfer any advertiser personal data outside EU/EEC? No. Outsourced hosting solution with Evry, servers located in Sweden, Microsoft licenses and cloud services with data centers in Ireland and Amsterdam 8. Do you have sub-processors? Yes. A list of data processors can be provided upon request. 9. Do you have an appointed DPO? Yes. Nick Morris, nick.morris@tradedoubler.com 10. Are your staff under any form of confidentiality clauses? Yes. Included in employment agreements that have to be signed by every employee at time of employment. Also confidentiality clauses in our advertiser agreements 11.

content: tradedoubler.com terms, you must immediately cease to use our service. The Publisher agreement states that Tradedoubler may change the terms by notifying publishers by e-mail or on TD’s website. if they don’t accept the changes, they must immediately cease to use the service, that’s how it’s regulated in the agreement (see section 2.4). 26. What if a publisher does not want to receive newsletters any more? GDPR stipulates the right to be forgotten, for example not to receive any direct marketing emails or newsletters anymore. We need to comply with that. GDPR does not specify how we do that. The best way to reassure a publisher will not receive newsletters is to tick the box “no” under “edit contact information”. Preferably themselves but if it does not work that the publisher teams assist them to do that 27. Does our newsletters need an unsubscribe link? The publisher has once ticked the box that they want to receive newsletters (consent) we do not need to add an “unsubscribe link” onto each newsletter we send acc to GDPR.

content: tradedoubler.com • GDPR Article 17 – Right to erasure (“right to be forgotten”) 1. The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay and the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies: (a) the personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected or otherwise processed; (b) the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6(1) the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes; (c) the data subject objects to the processing pursuant to Article 21(1) Right to object, and there are no overriding legitimate grounds for the processing. content: 01.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 2 tradedoubler .com 1. User Management Process User management for employees involves the following three processes: • User creation • Change of position/role • User termination The key players (departments) that are involved in user management process are: • General Manager/Head of Dept/Line Manager General Manager or Head of Dept is the overall responsible person for employees in a country or HQ Dept. A General Manager or Head of Dept can employ, reassign and terminate users. The role can be delegated to Line Managers within a country or HQ Dept. • Business Control and Compliance & Legal Director Compliance & Legal Director is responsible for the insider rules, policies, code for corporate governance etc. Financial access in BI is approved and given firstly by Head of Accounting or secondarily by Compliance & Legal Director . • Second Line Support (SLS) Second Line Support takes care of user administration in production systems (i.e. Spaceport/Guru).
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Access Right Management Standard 20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Access Right Management Standard 20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 2}

content: nting or secondarily by Compliance & Legal Director . • Second Line Support (SLS) Second Line Support takes care of user administration in production systems (i.e. Spaceport/Guru). • Corporate IT ( CIT) Corporate IT is responsible for Infrastructure and MS operations, Help desk portal and Application support. CIT administers users in internal systems (i.e. Microsoft Entra ID in Microsoft environment) and carries out users and devices administration in IT-systems. • IT Operations IT Operations maintain and control the production systems.

content: 01.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 3 tradedoubler .com 2 Workflows for User Access Management 2. 1 New User Creation Workflow Following is the workflow when the General Manager , Head of Dept or Line Manager wishes to create a new user . 1. The General Manager/ Head of Dept/ Line Manager must create a user profile in Personio. An automated script creates Microsoft accounts and email address for the new user . then fill in and submit the Request User Creation Form in: User Account Ordering Portal (UAOP) with at least the following information: • Name of the employee (first and last name) • Job Title • T radedoubler company/Business unit • Date of employment • Spaceport/Guru security roles • CRM access required or not 2. Once the “Request User Creation Form” is submitted, approval requests are sent to: a) Local General Manager for local Spaceport/Guru and/or BI access request b) Legal if multi-market Spaceport/Guru and/or BI access is requested. 3. . CRM and BI access are automatically added to the user if requested.

content: 1 General This policy describes the handling of payroll and publisher payment data within accounting to be compliant with the new General Data Protection Regulation. The data is collected and stored according to below for bookkeeping purposes. Payroll Payroll data files received via e-mail from Payroll partner should be saved on B/Financials/Salaries which is a folder that is restricted to access only for accountants at SSC in Stockholm. The e-mail received should be deleted when the files have been saved. No sensitive personal data are sent by email or is sent by secure email. Material kept in the folder on B/Financials shall be deleted according to the local rules for how long bookkeeping material has to be stored. If any of the material is printed it has to be stored in a safe place and when not neede d anymore be sorted in the destruction bin for privacy material. No material with personal data is to be stored in a binder.
evidence_reference: Accounting and Personal Data Policy_2018-05-18.pdf - Page 2

content: it has to be stored in a safe place and when not neede d anymore be sorted in the destruction bin for privacy material. No material with personal data is to be stored in a binder. Files received for upload in bank for payment should be stored temporarily on desktop, immediately when upload has been made in the bank solution the file should be deleted from the desktop. Destruction The destruction bins are exchanged and emptied once per year by an external party. The exchange of the locks to the destruction bins is supervised by the Office Manager. The external party is the only one with a key to the locks of the destruction bins. No external access to premises where destruction bins are kept. All printed documents including personal data must be thrown in the destruction bins immediately after usage. Printed documents including personal data must never be unattended. Documents including personal data that is temporarily stored on local desktop must be deleted immediately after upload in bank solution.

Publisher payment Publisher payment data that is retrieved in an excel file from Spaceport for bookkeeping should be saved on B/Financials/Affiliate payments which is restricted for access to accountants at SSC in Stockholm only. If any of the files are printed it has to be stored in a safe place and when not needed anymore be sorted in the destruction bin for privacy material according to above No material with personal data is to be stored in a binder or be attached to any internal invoice issued.

Third parties Withholding taxes regarding Tradedoubler employees are reported to local tax authorities according to local tax legislation by local payroll partners. Withholding taxes regarding publishers that are natural persons are reported to the Swedish and Norwegian tax authorities according to local tax legislation. The files to the Swedish and Norwegian tax authorities including social security numbers besides name, address and withholding taxes are downloaded by the Database team from Spaceport on a USB stick and then given to respective Accountant at SSC in Stockholm who upload the data in respective Tax Authories´reporting systems. The USB stick shall be stored in a safe place until no further need (confirmation from Tax Authorities ) and then all data on the USB stick should be deleted as soon as possible.

Deviations Deviations from the policy above: Tradedoubler Netherlands Payroll data are withdrawn from local payroll partner´s payroll software. Accesses to payroll software are restricted to staff who needs it to perform their duties. Withdrawn data files at SSC to be stored according to section 2. above. Tradedoubler Poland Payroll files from and to local payroll partner are downloaded and uploaded in payroll partner´s document sharing software. Accesses to payroll software are restricted to staff who needs it to perform their duties Payroll files are printed and stored in locked cupboard only accessible to local accountants after recording in bookkeeping and manual update in local bank solution . Tradedoubler Italy Payroll data is kept in payroll system protected by password and with limited access to HR staff at ICS. Local accountant at ICS does not have access to personal data.

content: 27 .05.2025 Stockholm Sweden tradedoubler .com 1 Overview Business Continuity Plan Approved by CEO 26 May 2025 About T radedoubler Established in 1999 in Stockholm by Felix Hagnö and Martin Lorentzon, the co-founder of Spotify, T radedoubler swiftly ascended to become Europe's no. 1 Affiliate Marketing network, listed on the Stockholm Stock Exchange since 2005. Presently, we extend our proprietary Partner Marketing and T ech Solutions across +90 countries, backed by a dedicated team exceeding 350 T radedoublers. While operating on a global scale, our ethos remains deeply rooted in local authenticity and a commitment to people-centric values. More information can be found on www.tradedoubler .com

content: 27 .05.2025 Stockholm Sweden tradedoubler .com 2 1 Overview T radedoubler has a commitment to its advertisers and owners to provide reliable and efficient services. T o fulfil this commitment, T radedoubler has established a Business Continuity Plan (BCP). The BCP contains a series of prepared actions to have at hand in a major crisis for T radedoubler business. These actions are geared toward achieving continuity of business-critical functions through organisational preparations, which includes IT-operations. Therefore, business-critical IT service suppliers are also in scope for the BCP at T radedoubler . The BCP aims to start planning for an alternative solution to ensure continuity for T radedoubler in case of failure of the supplier BCP . Since crisis situations at IT service suppliers can constitute a risk for T radedoubler , T radedoubler’s BCP also aims to aid suppliers in their BCP work. 2 BCP Structure In the event of disaster affecting one of T radedoubler’s IT service providers, the BCP is activated.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "BCP Short Version 2025-05-26.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\BCP Short Version 2025-05-26.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 2}

content: adedoubler’s BCP also aims to aid suppliers in their BCP work. 2 BCP Structure In the event of disaster affecting one of T radedoubler’s IT service providers, the BCP is activated. The focus for the T radedoubler BCP is prioritisation, communication and organization during a disaster . The BCP is divided into three phases, each phase has defined action points and decisions to be made. Figure 1. Overview of the steps and activities in the Business Continuity Plan
metadata_json: {"source_file": "BCP Short Version 2025-05-26.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\BCP Short Version 2025-05-26.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 2, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 2}

content: 27 .05.2025 Stockholm Sweden tradedoubler .com 3 3 BCP Initial Phase T radedoubler’s BCP is triggered by a disaster notification from a supplier . If the scenario described in the notification fulfils the criteria for a crisis the BCP is triggered, and the Crisis Management T eam are gathered. Their first tasks are to establish contact with the supplier or relevant internal department. An independent damage assessment is performed to assess which services are affected and the extent of the damage. It provides a basis for decisions regarding prioritisations and allocation of resources. The Crisis Management T eam’s responsibility is to coordinate actions and make decisions throughout the business continuity management process. All meetings and decisions during crisis management are documented. 4 BCP Action Phase In the Action Phase, the two main areas of focus are to aid the supplier as much as possible, and to continuously assess and handle the crisis accordingly.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "BCP Short Version 2025-05-26.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\BCP Short Version 2025-05-26.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 3", "unit_index": 3, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 3}

content: nted. 4 BCP Action Phase In the Action Phase, the two main areas of focus are to aid the supplier as much as possible, and to continuously assess and handle the crisis accordingly. The Crisis Management T eam holds regular meetings where the situation and need for Operative T eams are evaluated, decisions on prioritisations are made, and resources are allocated. The Crisis Management T eam also make decisions on whether to execute alternative solutions or not. During the Action Phase Operative T eams are created. Examples of Operative T eams and tasks are: • The Media and Communication T eam aims at synchronizing and planning information to different target groups internally and externally. • The Supplier Assistance Team supports the supplier in their BCP work with any needed resources. • The Alternative Solution Team investigates possibilities of a solution to the disaster and plans the execution of an alternative solution.

content: eir BCP work with any needed resources. • The Alternative Solution Team investigates possibilities of a solution to the disaster and plans the execution of an alternative solution. 5 BCP Alternative Solution Phase If the supplier fails to mitigate the disaster, the Crisis Management T eam may take the decision to execute an alternative solution. If so, the BCP enters the Alternative Solution Phase. The decision to execute an alternative solution is based on dialogues with the supplier, input from the Operative T eams, and assessments on the impact on T radedoubler’s business.

metadata_json: {"source_file": "Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 1", "unit_index": 1, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 1, "page": 1}

content: 01.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 2 tradedoubler .com General The purpose of the policy is to establish the goals and the vision for the breach response process. This policy will clearly define to whom it applies and under what circumstances, and it will include the definition of a breach, staff roles and responsibilities, standards, as well as reporting, remediation, and feedback mechanisms. The policy shall be made easily available to all employees whose duties involve personal data handling, data privacy and security protection. Tradedoubler is committed to protecting Tradedoubler's employees, partners and the company from illegal or damaging actions by individuals, either knowingly or unknowingly. Background This policy mandates that any TD staff who suspects that a theft, breach or exposure of personal data has occurred must immediately provide a description of what occurred via e -mail to dpo@tradedoubler.com. This is the responsibility of each Tradedoubler employee. This DPO e- mail is monitored by the DPO, Compliance & Legal Director and the CISO.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 2}

content: d via e -mail to dpo@tradedoubler.com. This is the responsibility of each Tradedoubler employee. This DPO e- mail is monitored by the DPO, Compliance & Legal Director and the CISO. The DPO group will investigate all reported thefts, data breaches and exposures to confirm if a theft, breach or exposure has occurred. If a theft, breach or exposure has occurred, the DPO group will follow the appropriate procedure in place. Scope This policy applies to all whom collect, access, maintain, distribute, process, protect, store, use, transmit, dispose of, or otherwise handle personally identifiable information of Tradedoubler prospects, advertisers, publishers or employees. What is a data breach? According to the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), a data breach is defined as a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, personal data transmitted, stored, shared or otherwise processed. Here are a few examples of data breaches under GDPR: 1.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 2, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 2}

content: oss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, personal data transmitted, stored, shared or otherwise processed. Here are a few examples of data breaches under GDPR: 1. Unauthorized Access: An employee accesses personal data without permission. For instance, a staff member looks at payroll records without a valid reason. 2. Accidental Disclosure: Personal data is sent to the wrong recipient. For example, an email containing sensitive information is mistakenly sent to the wrong person . 3. Lost or Stolen Devices: A laptop containing unencrypted personal data is lost or stolen.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 3, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 2}

content: 01.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 3 tradedoubler .com 4. Data Alteration: Personal data is altered without authorization. This might occur if someone changes customer information in a database without permission. 5. Data Destruction: Personal data is accidentally or unlawfully destroyed. For example, a company might accidentally delete customer records due to a software error. Confirmed theft, data breach or exposure of Personal data As soon as a theft, data breach or exposure containing personal data is identified, the process of removing all access to that resource will begin. The CISO or acting CISO will chair an incident response team to handle the breach or exposure. The team will include members from: • Technical Management Group (TMG) • CEO • CFO • Compliance & Legal Director • DPO • Marketing • Human Resources • The affected unit or department that uses the involved system or output or whose data may have been breached or exposed • Additional departments based on the data type involved, additional individuals as deemed necessary by the DPO group Confirmed theft, breach or exposure
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 3", "unit_index": 3, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 3}

content: e been breached or exposed • Additional departments based on the data type involved, additional individuals as deemed necessary by the DPO group Confirmed theft, breach or exposure of personal data The CEO will be notified of the theft, breach or exposure. TMG, along with the designated forensic team, will analyse the breach or exposure to determine the root cause. Breach Notification Any data breach containing personal data where Tradedoubler is the Data Controller should be reported to the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours after Tradedoubler has become aware of it - unless it is unlikely that it results in a risk for the data subject. Any data breach containing personal data where Tradedoubler is the Data Processor (advertiser or cashback publisher personal data) should be reported to the Data Controller (advertiser or cashback publisher) without undue delay. The breach notification should contain the following information: 1. The nature of the breach, 2. The number of data subjects concerned,
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 3", "unit_index": 3, "chunk_index": 2, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 3}

content: 01.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 4 tradedoubler .com 3. The categories and number of personal data concerned, 4. Name and contact details of the contact from whom more information can be obtained, 5. The likely consequences of the breach, 6. The measures taken to address the breach and measures to mitigate the possible adverse effects, if any. If the breach results in a high risk for the personal data subject, e.g. loss of control of their personal data, identity theft, financial loss etc, the data subject must also be notified of the data breach. The information should be given in a clear and plain language and at least include bullet points 4-6 above. The General Manager is responsible for data breach notifications to advertisers and relevant publishers (cashback publishers) within their market(s) and for keeping to the agreed notification period which may vary but typically should be as soon as you become aware o f a data breach. The DPO group is responsible for data breach reporting to respective data authorities.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Databreach Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 4", "unit_index": 4, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 4}

content: period which may vary but typically should be as soon as you become aware o f a data breach. The DPO group is responsible for data breach reporting to respective data authorities. The DPO group is also responsible for documenting each data breach in JIRA using the Data Breach Incident Management queue. . Enforcement Any Tradedoubler employees found in violation of this policy may be subject to disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment. Any third party partner company found in violation may have their network connection terminated. For any questions regarding this Policy or if you suspect or notice non compliance with this Policy please contact Compliance &Legal Director at privacy@tradedoubler.com.

content: 02.04.202501.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 2 tradedoubler .com General This policy is provided to ensure that operative actions by Tradedoubler´s advertiser and publisher facing employees comply with the GDPR regulations which came into effect in May 2018 when handling our partners´or employee personal data. This Policy also covers the retention periods of personal data collected by Tradedoubler. Advertisers and agencies Tradedoubler provides performance marketing and technology solutions for advertisers and agencies. As part of sales and account handling process, Tradedoubler captures and stores personal data for the contact person at the advertiser and agency to provide the service from a marketing and technical perspective. The personal data is also used as contact information in case of any issues and for financial services including invoicing. Advertisers and agencies are usually VAT registered companies with many employees. Advertiser and agency personal data will include full name, title, email address, landline and mobile phone numbers.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "GDPR Client Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\GDPR Client Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 2}

content: ncies are usually VAT registered companies with many employees. Advertiser and agency personal data will include full name, title, email address, landline and mobile phone numbers. Publishers Tradedoubler provides tracking and payment services for its network of publishers. As part of the service Tradedoubler provides, it is required to capture and store personal data for a contact person on each publisher to verify that each publisher is not fraudulent and to use as contact information in case of any issues and for financial services including payment and handling of tax. Publishers range from individuals to VAT registered companies with many employees. Publisher personal data includes full name, address, email address, date of birth and bank account details and if an individual also their personal identification number or similar.

content: 02.04.202501.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 3 tradedoubler .com What is GDPR and what does it mean to me? The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) (Regulation (EU) 2016/679) is a regulation by which the European Parliament, the Council of the European Union and the European Commission intend to strengthen and unify data protection for all individuals within the European Union (EU). GDPR requires that Tradedoubler handles and processes data in such a way as to not misuse or distribute that data to unauthorized internal and external parties and to only store that data for as long as is reasonably required. Direct Marketing & Sales The key rule for data handling in direct marketing and sales is that only information that is necessary to proceed with a business relationship is used and stored. Only emails found in the public domain or opted in via verified third parties and where consent has been obtained may be used for communication without prior permission. All correspondence should give the recipient the opportunity to opt out of all future correspondence.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "GDPR Client Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\GDPR Client Policy_20250331.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 3", "unit_index": 3, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 3}

content: sent has been obtained may be used for communication without prior permission. All correspondence should give the recipient the opportunity to opt out of all future correspondence. All personal data collected must be securely stored in the CRM, Sharepoint/Teams, your local elec and in Spaceport. Any offline items that contain personal data, such as contracts, correspondence and business cards, must be securely stored at all times in a locked cupboard or drawer when not in use. Each office should have a designated ‘secure’ storage cupboard for such data.This is the responsibility of each General Manager. When offline items containing personal data are not needed for future account management,sales purposes or any other purposes, they must be destroyed via the confidential documents bin, which is available in each office. Reviews and destruction should be made at least twice a year.

content: 02.04.202501.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 4 tradedoubler .com Advertiser & Publisher Management The same principle for data handling is true of advertiser and publisher management. Only personal data required to transact business should be stored. All offline references should be securely stored when not in use according to above. What data will you have access to? Only employees who require access to personal data to fulfill their work duties will be granted access to advertiser’s, agency’s or publisher´s personal data. Employees in the Client Development Teams, Sales teams, Finance team, Partner Development Teams, and Technical Support teams might require access to personal data to perform their work duties. Requests for other Tradedoubler employees to have access to personal data must be approved by General Manager,Head of Departments or by Compliance & Legal Director (several market access) in accordance with Tradedoubler Access Right Management Standard available in Sharepoint/Teams under Legal/GDPR.

content: 02.04.202501.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 5 tradedoubler .com Termination of employment or change of position Upon leaving Tradedoubler or change of position where access to advertiser or publisher personal data is no longer needed, all access to personal data shall be removed, as stipulated in Tradedoubler’s Access Right Management Standard. It is the responsibility of each Tradedoubler employee’s Line Manager to ensure that anyone with access to advertiser or publisher personal data has their access terminated upon end of their employment or change of position with Tradedoubler.

content: 02.04.202501.04.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 6 tradedoubler .com Right of access and right to be forgotten Information regarding any advertiser´s or publisher´s contact person should be provided as soon as possible and within one month. This applies for all requests for access and/or rectification of information from the advertiser’s or publisher’s contact person. The request should be answered by the responsible Client Developmen t or P artner Development Manager and in accordance with the document “Requests from Data Subjects Manual” available in Sharepoint /Teams under Legal/GDPR, link here. Individual’s, client´s contact person(s) as well as Tradedoubler employees also have the right to be forgotten, and as such all related information regarding that individual will be permanently removed from all systems by Tradedoubler on receipt of such request. This is provided that the information about the advertiser´s or publisher contact person is not necessary for future sales and/or account handling or must be stored according to local accounting rules or local tax legislation.

content: e advertiser´s or publisher contact person is not necessary for future sales and/or account handling or must be stored according to local accounting rules or local tax legislation. Retention periods Upon termination of employment by Tradedoubler staff, the contact details shall be deleted and replaced/updated with new contact person in our internal systems (Spaceport, CRM, Outlook, Sharepoint) within one month from termination date. This is the responsibility of each TD employee’s Line Manager. Other HR related data should be stored according to Handbook GDPR Human Resources which you can find here. Upon termination of advertiser relationship including Grow advertisers, based on the date of the final invoice, the personal data and the advertiser´s contact details should be reviewed every six months after termination and be deleted if not deemed necessary for future sales and/or account handling. This is the responsibility of the DPO.

This is the responsibility of the DPO. Compliance For any questions regarding this Policy or if you suspect or notice non compliance with this Policy please contact Compliance & Legal Director at privacy@tradedoubler.com or DPO at dpo@tradedoubler.com. content: Human Resources & Personal Data Policy Adopted by CFO 2 July, 2018
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 1", "unit_index": 1, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 1, "page": 1}

content: 1 1. General Personal data is something related to individuals, which is strongly connected to all HR areas. We need to be aware of how we handle personal data in all steps and processes, including recruitments, employments, changes during the employment and upon termination of employment – basically the whole cycle. All kind of data that can relate to individuals (personal number, name, gender, family members etc.) is and need to be handled as personal data. This handbook describes the handling within three areas as well as collection, processing, storage and deletion in all areas: • Recruitment • Employment • Terminated employment We always need to relate to” if we need the data and information” in all matters when it comes to IF we need to save all kind of personal data – if not, there is no reason to store/save data. If the answer is no, to the question: is there a reason saving this info – the data should be deleted and not be saved what so ever. Don´t save/keep emails consisting of personal data in your inbox.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 2}

content: o, to the question: is there a reason saving this info – the data should be deleted and not be saved what so ever. Don´t save/keep emails consisting of personal data in your inbox. If there is no purpose or legal basis saving data – make sure this is deleted. In general, we need to inform applicants, employees etc. the purpose of why we saving the personal data related to the person. The legal basis needs to be stated. It is our responsibility to make sure personal data is up to date. Therefore, we should ask employees frequently if their personal data is correct and up to date. Once per year this should be updated, and it is each manager’s responsibility to make sure we are compliant. 2. Recruitment 2.1 Information to applicants Inform applicants when receiving their application. The applicant should be informed how we store and manage their application. Inform applicants in the job ad, that no references are needed in this stage.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 2, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 2}

content: ceiving their application. The applicant should be informed how we store and manage their application. Inform applicants in the job ad, that no references are needed in this stage. Remember that references are personal data, and if we receive this information – the hiring manager needs to inform references that we have their personal details/contact information.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 3, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 2}

content: 2 2.2 Delete and appeal When the recruitment process is closed, make sure all CV´s and other documents are deleted – both digital and paper wise. If any of the material is printed it has to be stored in a safe place and when not needed anymore be sorted in the destruction bin for privacy material. No material with personal data is to be stored in a binder. The applicant should “mark in a box” when applying to vacancy´s, and give confirmation in sharing the CV and other documents. There the applicant is informed how we handle personal data and the recruitment selection. 2.3 How we handle CV and other documents CV´s and all documents related to recruitments should only be handled of the hiring manager. If hiring managers for any reason would like to keep/save the CV for future vacancy’s, the manager needs to have the approval from the applicant. If any of the material is printed it has to be stored in a safe place and when not needed anymore be sorted in the destruction bin for privacy material. No material with personal data is to be stored in a binder.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 3", "unit_index": 3, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 3}

content: d it has to be stored in a safe place and when not needed anymore be sorted in the destruction bin for privacy material. No material with personal data is to be stored in a binder. Sensitive personal data such as personal identification number or equivalent, sick leave data, religion etc. included in CVs, employment agreements or payroll files must not be sent by email. It must be handled by either document sharing or encrypted emails. When receiving spontaneous applications – make sure we have the candidate’s approval for saving the data. 3. Employment 3.1 Employment contract and information to the employee Information we gather and save is information that “we really need” to perform our work duties and/or fulfill employment agreement and/or local labor laws and/or local tax legislation. There must be a clearly stated purpose for collecting and handling personal data and it may not be used for anything else than the defined purpose. The employment agreement is signed in two copies, and the copy Tradedoubler keep needs to be saved in a locked cabinet.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 3", "unit_index": 3, "chunk_index": 2, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 3}

content: may not be used for anything else than the defined purpose. The employment agreement is signed in two copies, and the copy Tradedoubler keep needs to be saved in a locked cabinet. It should only be Country Manager/Department Manager and local HR where available who have access to original employee contracts and additional agreement. When additional agreements which includes personal data (ACE, Bonus agreements, performance appraisals etc.) are signed, this needs to be kept in locked cabinet. All digital copies are sent to local HR by encrypted emails, centrally, and kept at local HR system/server with limited access to local and central HR.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 3", "unit_index": 3, "chunk_index": 3, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 3}

content: 3 Information how we save and gather personal data needs to be shared to the employee. All templates that the employees fill in is only including data that is related to employment only. When we collect and save personal data related to in case of emergency details – this needs to be informed to the person who the employee named as the in case of emergency contact by local HR where available and Office Manager for other markets. Make sure we cover which personal data we collect and save and for what purpose, in the employment agreement. 3.2 Information by email When employment information needs to be shared by email, this email needs to be within an encrypted email. Only necessary information is shared by email, as for example information related to new hires, sick leaves, performance, change of manager, salary, resignations, terminations, labor law or similar. 4. Terminated employment 4.1 Deleting policy In general, all information that is not needed or shared with local HR should be deleted in destruction bins.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Handbook GDPR Human Resources_2018-07-02.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 4", "unit_index": 4, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 4}

content: ns, labor law or similar. 4. Terminated employment 4.1 Deleting policy In general, all information that is not needed or shared with local HR should be deleted in destruction bins. All documents and personal data to employees should be reviewed every six months and if not longer needed for HR management purposes to be deleted. 5. Retention period In general, personal data related to applicants, employees or former employees should be deleted if there is no labor law or tax legislation purposes. To make sure we only have the needed personal data, retention reviews are done every six months by manager and local and central HR. 6. Compliance For any questions regarding this Policy or if you suspect or notice noncompliance with this Policy please contact Compliance Responsible at privacy@tradedoubler.com Noncompliance with this Policy can render a warning or other disciplinary actions.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 2 tradedoubler.com 1 Information Classification Guidelines All stored information within Tradedoubler must be classified. The objective with information classification is to estimate the need for information protection. The need for such protection will judge how information is handled in telephone, e - mail, mail, fax, USB, PC at business trips or other use of the PC outside T radedoubler, printouts and maculation. Information classification is also important to protect the interest of our client s, owners/shareholders, local business units, employees, business partners or suppliers. Note: Personal data shall always be classified and handled as “confidential”. When managing information that can contain personal data, the following policies shall be considered in addition: • External Privacy Policy (when managing client and user’s personal data) • Accounting & Personal Data Policy (when handling payroll and publisher payment data) • GDPR Client Policy (when dealing with advertisers and publishers employees personal data) • Human Resources & Personal Data

Information classification should take the following into account: • Sensitive client information as prices, agreements, traffic data, and other client data. • Legal requirements, e.g. privacy regulations (GDPR, see above) • Owners’/shareholders’ interests and • Stock market regulations regarding financial reporting. • Partner/subcontractor information. • Image and trademark values. • Loss of patent rights/abilities. • Loss of business or business opportunities. • Competitor interests. • Product, design and research information.

content: 08. 10.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 2 tradedoubler .com Information Security Policy The CEO is ultimately responsible for the information security within the T radedoubler Group. The day-to-day management of security matters is delegated to the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO). The Engineering Leadership is responsible for the IT production and can therefore not act as CISO. The Engineering Leadership is responsible for strategic IT decisions and daily IT operations and implementing all agreed information security measures. The CISO shall closely monitor and follow up the Product Department’s work to ensure and improve quality of the information security. The CISO shall regularly report to the CTO or the GMT how the information security work is pursued, the preparedness for disturbances and all actions needed. The ISO 27001 framework and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) are the basis for the information security work within T radedoubler . The information security work shall be preventing as well as controlling.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Policy 2025-10-08.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Policy 2025-10-08.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 2}

content: l Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) are the basis for the information security work within T radedoubler . The information security work shall be preventing as well as controlling. New IT projects must be evaluated with regard to security including the GDPR regulation before starting the project and continuously be controlled regarding security during development, start of operation and management. The CISO is responsible for recurrent reviews of framework, applications, achieved security level, reports and the protection of personal data. The result of these reviews shall be reported to the CTO and, if required, made available to the external auditors. The CISO shall monitor that the organisation’s demands are supplied by security defence, contingency plans and education. The Products Department shall provide a budget for information security actions and make sure that all employees have adequate information security education. The CISO shall be informed about all major and material changes to the IT infrastructure.

The ambition is that this classification will be supported in the IT systems used. The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is an integrated part of T radedoubler’s information security work. Management of personal data is regulated through policies. These describes how the handling of personal data is to be performed within T radedoubler . Established policies provide coverage of the management of payroll and publisher payment data, data breaches, client data, human resources and other personal data as well as an external privacy policy. content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 2 tradedoubler.com 1 Introduction Tradedoubler has an information security framework set up in order to protect the company, its customers and its employees from potential risks. The framework contains measures to ensure that confidentiality, integrity and availability of information is ma intained. This security framework is applicable to all Tradedoubler employees, internal consultants and suppliers. The ambition and principles for information security work within Tradedoubler is stated in the information security policy, along with roles and responsibilities. The standards in the framework contain rules set up to accomplish the goals and principles st ated in the information security policy. Guidelines in the framework provide direction within information classification and when using portable devices for accessing the Tradedoubler IT environment. Topics relat ed to personal data and GDPR are covered by a number of policies and a handbook, each addressing personal data in different context s .

content: he Tradedoubler IT environment. Topics relat ed to personal data and GDPR are covered by a number of policies and a handbook, each addressing personal data in different context s . A data breach policy is established, addressing any breach of personal data. The rules in the framework must be followed by all employees to ensure that information is processed and modified correctly, including personal data. In the case of violations of the information security framework rules, Tradedoubler reserves the right to exercise disciplinary actions which ultimately could result in termination of employment. For any queries regarding the content of this document or interpretation of certain rules, the nearest manager or CISO should be contacted. 1.1 Information Security Compliance with ISO 27001 Tradedoubler follows the security practices and framework set up in the ISO 27001 standard.

The policy sets up guiding principles and responsibilities for information security related work within Tradedoubler. The CEO is ultimately responsible for the information security within the Tradedoubler Group. The day - to - day management of security matters is delegated to the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO). The Engineering Leadership is responsible for the IT production . According to the policy , all information security related incidents should be reported to the CISO, Compliance & Legal Directo r and the DPO . Further all employees should receive security education within the company.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 4 tradedoubler.com 2 Organization of Information Security Areas for which assigned roles are responsible are stated in this section. 2.1 System Owners’ Responsibility 2 .1.1: System owners are responsible for the overall security of the ir system. This responsibility also includes controls, log reviews, access control audits and proposed actions when deviances are detected , as well as adherence to this Information Security Standard. 2.2 System Administrators’ Responsibility Besides the responsibilities set upon the system owner, the system administrators have a greater knowledge of the individual IT system. 2 .2.1: System administrators are responsible to set security rules, enforce access control, access logging as well as to implement minimum security baselines.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 5 tradedoubler.com 3 Mobile Devices A mobile device (smartphone, tablets and laptops) represents an asset consisting of its hardware, its information content and its abilities to connect to Tradedoubler’s network. All these factors may present a risk for the company. All rules concerning lap tops are also applicable to advanced mobiles or other portable or stationary computers. Two categories of mobile devices are used within Tradedoubler’s IT environment. These are: • Tradedoubler owned , and • Privately owned. Privately owned devices are allowed to connect to Tradedoubler’s network, if they satisfy the security requirements listed below. Users are responsible for their mobile devices, and that they satisfy all the requirements. To comply with Tradedoubler data protection policy all mobile device s must have Intune Company Portal installed and connected. 3.1 Security Requirements for Mobile Devices ( laptops, smartphones, tablets) 3.1.1 : All devices must have Intune Company Portal installed and connected. 3.1.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 5", "unit_index": 5, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 5}

content: alled and connected. 3.1 Security Requirements for Mobile Devices ( laptops, smartphones, tablets) 3.1.1 : All devices must have Intune Company Portal installed and connected. 3.1. 2 : Released patches must always be applied to mobile devices within one week of their release. If the user administers his/her own device, the user is responsible for patching the mobile device. 3 .1. 3 : Laptops and phones are not allowed to be left unattended on desks outside ordinary work hours. Mobile devices must be carried home or placed in locked cabinets. 3.1. 4 : All sensitive data must be backed up on a SharePoint/Teams repository or on a, by Tradedoubler, appointed solution (e.g., cloud service). 3 .1. 5 : The use of non - approved cloud - based applications to backup Tradedoubler’s data is not allowed. 3 .1. 6 : All laptops must be password protected. 3 .1. 7 : Laptop password shall follow the general password directives regarding complexity (see requirement 6.3.1 ).

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 6 tradedoubler.com 3 .1. 8 : Mobile phones and tablets shall have screen lock and require at least PIN or biometric authentication for log - in . The PIN shall consist of at least 6 digits. 3 .1. 9 : All mobile devices must only contain licensed software, acquired by either the owner or the company for whom the owner works. 3 .1. 10 : All mobile devices must have screen lock activated, with a password/PIN/biometric verification after a maximum of 5 minutes inactivity. 3 .1.1 1 : All smartphones and tablets must have an account lockout threshold, locking the devices after maximum 10 failed login attempts. 3 .1.1 2 : All mobile devices must use full disk encryption. For laptops the password must satisfy the general password requirements regarding complexity. 3 .1.1 3 : If any confidential information is stored directly on the mobile devices, then it must be separately encrypted (AES 256 with a password satisfying the general password requirement).

The antivirus software must be updated daily with the most recent definitions, have real - time protection activated and a scheduled scan shall be performed on a weekly basis. 3 .1.1 5 : The loss of any mobile device must be reported to the nearest manager within one day. The following information should be given: • Circumstances about the loss. • If the mobile devices were Tradedoubler owned or personal. • Crime Reference Number (if equipment is stolen). • Current information stored on the laptop. • If the information was encrypted or not. • A copy of the police report, if already made.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 8 tradedoubler.com 4 Human Resource Security This section ensures that Tradedoubler and its employees are aware of and fulfil their information security responsibilities. 4.1 Legal Issues Tradedoubler systems handle information regarding company advertisers and their customers which often are private persons. Regardless of the sensitivity of the stored information, Tradedoubler employees should treat all data that can contain personal data as confidential and may not deliver nor share this information to external parties without written approval from Compliance & Legal Director or DPO . 4 .1.1: Tradedoubler employees are not allowed to disclose information collected in Tradedoubler’s systems. 4.2 Trade Secrets Tradedoubler’s trade secrets as well as other customer’s and business partner’s trade secrets are protected by law. 4 .2.1: Tradedoubler employees are not allowed to disclose confidential information belonging to Tradedoubler, its customers or its business partners to other parties.

See User Termination Workflow in the Access Right Management Standard . 4 .3.2: All information produced or managed by the employee should remain the intellectual property of Tradedoubler. 4.4 Email and Internet Use When using and communicating through Tradedoubler computer systems employees are expected to act responsibly and respectfully. Every site visited or email sent from an

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 9 tradedoubler.com internal system will be registered as if sent by Tradedoubler. Some private use of internet is accepted if it does not cause any interference with work assignments . Note , if employees visit unsuitable web sites or send malicious emails, it is likely that this will reflect badly on Tradedoubler. Therefore, the following rules are established to protect employees and Tradedoubler itself. 4 .4.1: Employees must act responsibly and ethically when representing Tradedoubler through means of electronic communication. 4 .4.2 : Employees should not use Tradedoubler ´ s logotypes, office document layout or electronic mail with Tradedoubler signatures in private correspondence or writings. Tradedoubler’s logotype and business document layouts are for official use only. 4 .4.3: D ownloading of files or programs is not accepted if not to be used for work purposes. If there are uncertainties on whether a program or file can be downloaded, users should register a case at Helpdesk portal.

content: ams is not accepted if not to be used for work purposes. If there are uncertainties on whether a program or file can be downloaded, users should register a case at Helpdesk portal. Each employee is personally responsible for what they download. 4 .4.4: Employees should always follow Tradedoubler corporate standards regarding electronic mail use, including the use of formal language, proper choice of recipients, omission of either outdated or improper content and the use of confidentiality procedures inc luding the use of encryption (See Tradedoubler’s Information Classification Guidelines ). 4 .4.5: Employees are not allowed to visit internet sites with sexual ly explicit, prejudice, illegal or otherwise inappropriate content using Tradedoubler computer systems or network resources. 4 .4.6: Tradedoubler has the right to monitor all access to Internet sites from its internal network, to block access to Internet sites and to take disciplinary actions if rules are not followed. 4 .4.7: Tradedoubler owns any communication sent via email or that is stored on company equipment.

Management and other authorized staff have the right to access any material in employee email or computer at any time. Employees should not consider electronic communication, storage or access to be private if it is created or stored at work. 4.5 Safe AI Use 4.5. 1: E mployees must only use AI tools approved by Tradedoubler. 4.5.2: Only information classified as Internal (or lower) is allowed to be put into AI tools.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 10 tradedoubler.com 4.5.3: End users are responsible for the accuracy of AI - generated content. All AI - generated output must be reviewed and verified by a human before use. Automated responses, such as automated processes, AI - generated email or code, require human approval before execution. 4.5.4: Tradedoubler’s information must not be used to train external AI models. 4.5.5: Sensitive p ersonal data must not be used in AI - tools . 4.6 Personal and User Data Protection (GDPR) D ata protection laws must be considered when handling with data relating to individuals (personal data). Foremost is the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) applicable . Personal d ata is defined as all information that directly or indirectly may identify a living individual . P rocessing of p ersonal d ata is almost anything that can be done with personal data , from collecting it , recording, using, storing, sharing, transferring, amending and destroying it - or simply having it. Personal data shall always be classified as “confidential information”.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 10", "unit_index": 10, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 10}

content: ing it , recording, using, storing, sharing, transferring, amending and destroying it - or simply having it. Personal data shall always be classified as “confidential information”. The below listed documents contain Tradedoubler ’s goals, ambitions and requirements with regards to personal data and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR): • External Privacy Policy (client, publisher, web visitor and user’s personal data). • Accounting & Personal Data Policy (payroll and publisher payment data). • GDPR Client Policy (advertisers and publisher’s employee personal data). • Human Resources & Personal Data Policy (applicant and employee personal data). Each of the above document deals with the processing of personal data in different contexts. Any requirements described in the listed documents must be followed by all Tradedoubler personnel.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 11 tradedoubler.com 5 Asset Management The purpose of asset management is to identify organizational assets and define suitable protection responsibilities. 5.1 Information Classification and Handling The Information classification guidelines for Tradedoubler is approved by the Group CEO and published on Tradedoubler’s SharePoint / Teams in a separate document. For laptops, data media, e - mail communication as well as network access the Information classification guidelines states rules about the necessary level of protection for information that is communicated or stored. Examples of information requiring the highest level of classification (confidential) are personal data, financial reports, board material and security function sensitive data. H andling routines for personal data are described in the following polices (depending on processing purposes): • External Privacy Policy (when managing client and user’s personal data). • Accounting & Personal Data Policy (when handling payroll and publisher payment data).
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 11", "unit_index": 11, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 11}

content: essing purposes): • External Privacy Policy (when managing client and user’s personal data). • Accounting & Personal Data Policy (when handling payroll and publisher payment data). • GDPR Client Policy (when dealing with advertisers and publisher’s employee personal data). • Human Resources & Personal Data Policy (when managing applicants and employee’s personal data). The handling of personal data according to these policies are designed to ensure compliances with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). 5.2 Computer and Cloud Assets The goal is that Tradedoubler at any time shall be able to list all active cloud entities under its responsibility and control. 5.2.1: CTO is responsible for that an updated list of all current IT - assets can be shown upon request. The list shall contain at least: • Name of the asset. • IP - address or other unique ID for the asset. • Responsible vendor for the asset (e.g., Amazon, Google, etc.).
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 11", "unit_index": 11, "chunk_index": 2, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 11}

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 12 tradedoubler.com 6 Access Control Access control rules define rights and restrictions for system owners, system administrators and users towards Tradedoubler assets, to limit access to information. 6.1 Administrative Passwords System administrators should never allow end users to have less secure passwords than stated under the user responsibilities section. Further , the system administrator should use a strong password for administrative purposes. 6 .1.1: Systems administrators should enforce the use of MFA or strong passwords in systems. 6 .1.2: An administrative password should at least be 16 characters in length and should include at least one number , one special character, and consist of random characters. 6.2 Least Privilege Principle Tradedoubler seeks to comply with the least privilege principle. All logins should be conducted with ordinary user privilege and privilege escalation should be used for only those commands that require administrative rights and are allowed to be executed b y the user.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 12", "unit_index": 12, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 12}

content: nducted with ordinary user privilege and privilege escalation should be used for only those commands that require administrative rights and are allowed to be executed b y the user. Each system owner is responsible for ensur ing that the least privilege principle is maintained in their system. 6 .2.1: Systems owners should ensure that systems permit the use of least privilege principle and that its use is mandatory. 6 .2.2: System administrators should ensure that normal users have access only to the data they need for their job. 6 .2.3: System administrators should ensure that normal users log on only with their own credentials . 6.2.4: System administrators should only use privilege escalation for administrative tasks that are allowed to be executed by the user. 6.3 User Password and Account Management Passwords are the primary control that Tradedoubler uses to protect information and system resources. Cyber a ttacks are often successful due to the existence of weak password s within the system.

content: e primary control that Tradedoubler uses to protect information and system resources. Cyber a ttacks are often successful due to the existence of weak password s within the system. Even if administrative rules could be set to require a certain complexity, length and a change interval, the user is still responsible for good password management.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 12", "unit_index": 12, "chunk_index": 3, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 12}

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 13 tradedoubler.com A password should preferably consist of random characters (example: 1AxLp#T+2c). If the need exists to give out any password to someone within Tradedoubler , the password should be configured to be changed directly at the next logon. Passwords are a very common target for social engineering attacks, where a person pretends to be someone else asking for a user’s password. Users must never hand out their password to someone else in any kind of communication. 6 .3.1: P assword s must be longer than 10 characters and include at least one number and one special character and consist of random characters. 6.3.2: Multi - factor authentication ( MFA ) shall be used where possible. 6.3. 3 : Personal password should never be given out . S ystem administrators can reset a password, but they have no need for asking a password. 6 .3. 4 : Passwords to Tradedoubler’s different web applications should not be stored in any browsers input completion functionality; it is recommended to use a password manager program to store passwords instead. 6 .3.

content: ifferent web applications should not be stored in any browsers input completion functionality; it is recommended to use a password manager program to store passwords instead. 6 .3. 5 : Passwords should never be written down on reminder notes or stored in clear text. 6.3. 6 : Employees shall immediately use the report message function of their email client or register in the Helpdesk portal if they receive a suspicious looking email asking for their username and/or password. 6.4 Remote Access Connection Points External connections for incoming traffic are typically set up as firewall, VPN - gateways, terminal services or similar technologies. It is possible to set remote access point with the help of external computers or by opening an internal computer to an exte rnal network with the help of an additional cellular or wireless network access card. 6 .4.1: Any type of remote access point (for example SSH) must be approved by the Head of IT Operations.

content: rk with the help of an additional cellular or wireless network access card. 6 .4.1: Any type of remote access point (for example SSH) must be approved by the Head of IT Operations. 6.5 Remote Access Security and Authentication A remote workstation, such as a home computer or mobile device, could set Tradedoubler’s network at risk if the computer has low security and is affected by a virus. 6 .5.1: Remote access clients must comply with the Tradedoubler security standards. These includes the requirements placed on mobile devices (see chapter 3).
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 13", "unit_index": 13, "chunk_index": 3, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 13}

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 14 tradedoubler.com 6 .5.2: Remote logins to Tradedoubler’s network are allowed only via a secure VPN connection having strong authentication mechanism (e.g. combination of IP address plus password or two factor authentication.) 6 .5.3: When setting up a VPN connection to Tradedoubler, the default gateway must be set to go through Tradedoubler for all internet access. 6 .5.4: Storing company documents on home computers is not allowed. If a remote connection is made, any documents handled must remain on Tradedoubler network. Exceptions may be made for public documents.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 15 tradedoubler.com 7 Change Management Change management is the process behind planning, testing, executing and reviewing changes to Tradedoubler’s IT environment and systems. 7 .1: All actions during change management shall be documented, including the change request, the testing and the approval. The documentation shall be performed in Jira . 7.2: Change requests shall include information about the nature of the change, rationale, impact assessment, and urgency . 7.3: Change tests shall include issues and solutions as appropriate in the development cycle. Testing of changes shall be performed in an environment similar to the production environment . 7.4: Changes shall be approved prior being implemented in the production environment. Changes are approved by the VP Product or the Head of Operations. 7.5: Segregation of duties shall be ensured during change management, it shall not be the same person that developes, approves and implement the change.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 16 tradedoubler.com 8 Physical Security End user’s computers should be placed in locations where only employees or registered visitors have access. 8 . 1 : Workstation computers should be placed in access - controlled areas or protected by markings, engravings or similar protection. 8 . 2 : Fire detections systems must be installed in computer rooms. 8 . 3 : Computer rooms must have fire extinguishing systems adapted for computer rooms, i.e. Carbon dioxide or Halon substitutes. 8 . 4 : Controls must be in place to prevent water damage to computer equipment in computer rooms. At least water pipes should be isolated, and floor/cabinets raised to prevent damage to computer equipment.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 16", "unit_index": 16, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 1, "page": 16}

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 17 tradedoubler.com 9 Operations Security The following section describes measures by Tradedoubler to protect against malware and data loss. 9 .1 Systems and Vulnerability Scans/Penetration Test Most modern systems have internal commands and tools for audit and compliance scanning. It is the responsibility of a system owner and system administrator to select such functions or tools, to select settings and objectives and then to conduct these scann ings. 9 .1.1: Vulnerability scans of external facing systems/IP - adresses belonging to Treadedoubler’s asset list, must be performed monthly and the results presented at Tradedoubler Security Council. 9 .1.2: Extended external vulnerability scans, including web application vulnerability scans, shall be performed every quarter and the results presented at Tradedoubler Security Council. 9 .1.3: Pen etration tests and credentialed scan shall be decided adhoc by Tradedoubler Security C ouncil. They must be performed a t least annually.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 17", "unit_index": 17, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 2, "page": 17}

content: Tradedoubler Security Council. 9 .1.3: Pen etration tests and credentialed scan shall be decided adhoc by Tradedoubler Security C ouncil. They must be performed a t least annually. 9 .2 System Updates All systems should be updated on a regular basis and according to the system supplier’s recommendations. Critical updates for clients should be applied within a week from the patch being available allowing for a functionality testing and release of the upd ate within internal deployment schemes. For assets in the cloud, the same requirements apply. 9 .2.1: Systems administrators should test and apply critical workstation’s updates within one week. 9 .2.2: Systems administrators should on a monthly basis identify workstations that fail to apply critical updates and determine the reason of failure. 9 .2.3: Systems administrators should apply critical updates to production environments connected to the Internet within a month. 9 .2.4: System administrators shall test and apply updates to other environments within at least two months .

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 18 tradedoubler.com 9 .3 Central Data Backup Tradedoubler ’ s IT providers/ cloud hosting partners take back up of all assets according to standard routines, for each asset a predefined backup plan should be followed. 9. 3.1: The following systems shall have the following backup frequency and retention times: System Backup frequency Retention time Data bases daily Daily: 90 Weekly: N/A Monthly: 6 Yearly: 0 Financial syste m s daily Daily: 365 Weekly: N/A Monthly: 6 Yearly: 0 CRM daily Daily:30 Weekly: N/A Monthly: N/A Yearly: N/A 9 .3.2: Systems processing financial data shall be backed up according to each country’s financial legislation. 9 .3. 3 : System owners are responsible to ensure that backup schemas meet BCP objectives and a total loss of data could be handled. 9 .3. 4 : System administrators are responsible to ensure that backup run has executed correctly and that all data has been backed up. Restore tests should be made by Tradedoubler. 9 .3.

content: 4 : System administrators are responsible to ensure that backup run has executed correctly and that all data has been backed up. Restore tests should be made by Tradedoubler. 9 .3. 5 : Media taken offsite should either be encrypted or protected by locked case or other suitable protection. 9 .4 Local Data Backup Users are responsible for handling backups of any documents on their hard drives. Users should save any documents that are important for their day - to - day work on the Microsoft SharePoint/Teams provided by Tradedoubler. Any user of laptops should use the au tomatic synchronization functionality in Microsoft OneDrive to synchronize their desktop and documents directories with the cloud because of the inherent risk of the laptop being stolen.

Further they are responsible to copy these data files and document to Microsoft SharePoint/Teams or appointed cloud services where they will be backed up.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 20 tradedoubler.com 10 Communications Security Tradedoubler corporate network are provided for corporate applications. To provide a secure and reliable network , rules concerning network equipment and connection settings must be followed. 10 .1 Network Standards Tradedoubler expects all workstation to follow standard DHCP settings and do not assign IP - addresses, subnet masks or default gateways manually. 10 .1.1: Only TCP/IP version 4 is allowed across the corporate network. 10 .1.2: All equipment directly connected to the network should have been approved by the Corporate IT - department. This is valid for all computers, printers or other equipment with networking capabilities. 10 .1.3: All allocation of IP - addresses within the corporate network should be made by the IT Operations department. 10 .1.4: All external network encryption must be encrypted using standard encryption protocols.
