content: dresses within the corporate network should be made by the IT Operations department. 10 .1.4: All external network encryption must be encrypted using standard encryption protocols. 10 .2 Wireless Networks As wireless components are cheap the risk increases that local branch offices or local users bring wireless equipment, wireless access points or other wireless equipment and attach them to the corporate network. 10 .2.1: Only Tradedoubler Corporate IT department is allowed to set wireless networks within the corporate network. 10 .2.3: Tradedoubler has the right to define a wireless connection policy for any laptop or by administrative rules limit the networks to which access is allowed

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 21 tradedoubler.com 1 1 Compliance The following rules ensure that within Tradedoubler, copyright is not violated, and legal use of software is defined. 1 1 .1 Software Requirements Software distributors require that customer licensing and end user agreements should be kept. In addition, there is a possibility that a software package comes with malicious code. Therefore, it is crucial that users do not install software themselves, but instead contact Corporate IT if they need to have a particular software installed. 1 1 .1.1: Licensed programs installed on Tradedoubler’s computers are not allowed to be copied to other computers or to be used on home computer without approval by nearest manager. 1 1 .1.2: Commercial licensed programs obtained elsewhere are not allowed to be installed on company computers. All programs installed on Tradedoubler owned computers must be bought and licensed by Tradedoubler. 1 1 .2 Private M ultimedia F iles 1 1 .2.1: Only legally bought media files can be stored on the user’s workstation.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 22 tradedoubler.com 1 2 Cloud S ervice P roviders This chapter regards cloud services providers, including procurement of new cloud service providers and secure use of cloud services. 1 2 .1 Procurement of C loud S ervice P roviders 1 2 .1.1: New cloud service providers must be vetted by Tradedoubler and a due diligence performed before any contract is signed. The due diligence shall consider security controls related to physical, technical, and organizational security measures and compare the m to security requirements defined in Tradedoubler’s ISMS. The result of the vetting and due diligence shall be approved by CISO and Compliance & Legal Director. 1 2 .1.2: Upon decision to procure a new cloud service provider, an agreement with the provider shall define the allocation of incident management responsibilities between the supplier and Tradedoubler. The responsibilities must be aligned with Tradedoubler’s incid ent management process.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Information Security Standard 20260312.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 22", "unit_index": 22, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 22}

content: the allocation of incident management responsibilities between the supplier and Tradedoubler. The responsibilities must be aligned with Tradedoubler’s incid ent management process. 1 2 .1.3: A contract between Tradedoubler and a cloud service provider must include requirements on compliance with GDPR and other data protection regulations. Tradedoubler must be allowed to audit the cloud/SaaS vendor to verify its compliance. The vendor may also provide one of the following reports/certification covering the services used by Tradedoubler to avoid a direct audit: • ISO 27001 certification • SOC2/SOC3 – report • ISAE 3402 Type 2 – report 1 2 .1.4: If personal data covered by GDPR is stored at resources belonging to the cloud vendor, the data must be physically stored within the EU or by EU approved countries. If sensitive personal data is stored at the cloud vendor, the cloud vendor and its resourc es must be owned by entities within EU or approved countries.

content: Tradedoubler.com 1. Introduction The Tradedoubler Information Security Standard is mainly focused on securing Microsoft Windows based equipment managed by CIT. Since some users are having own or Tradedoubler owned Mac or Linux, this document contains instruction on how to make these devices compliant to the Information Security Standard. Only requirements that concerns deviations to the standard are listed in this document. The other requirements are to be found in the Tradedoubler Information Security Standard. 2. Interpretations of specific points for MacOS and Linux 5.1.1: Released patches must always be applied to Mobile Devices within one week of their release. The user is responsible to check for and update Mac and Linux computers accordingly. 5.1.3: The user is responsible for backing up his/hers device. Mac: Use TimeMachine to an external hard drive or a backed up network drive at Tradedoubler. Linux: The user is responsible for finding a suitable backup solution to his/her liking fulfilling the requirements of backing up Tradedoubler data.

For laptops the password must satisfy the general password requirements regarding complexity.

content: tradedoubler.com Integrity Policy Personal data of employees This is to inform you that Tradedoubler collects and stores personal data about you as your employer. The purpose of collecting and storing personal data about you are for several reasons/purposes, mainly: • To fulfill our employment contracts including paying out correct salaries • To comply with local labor laws • To comply with local tax legislation such as paying and reporting correct taxes to tax authorities • To enable your work duties and cooperation within Tradedoubler and with external parties by stating your contact details and titles/roles According to GDPR article 15.1 e, you have the right to: - Rectification - Erasure - Restriction - Objection of your personal data.

content: tradedoubler.com Photos of employees and partners Background The main business objective of Tradedoubler is to offer an affiliate marketing network, our key business is to bring people together: advertisers, publishers and Tradedoubler employees. Our business is a people business, therefor we wish to display our staff both internally and externally. Our employees are our most important asset and we are proud of them. Therefor we display them on our website and in our marketing collateral. The pictures reflect and give a different value of how we want to be perceived: for employees, potential employees, advertisers and publishers. We deem that pictures show in a better way what Tradedoubler is and what we do as an employer or business partner - easier to understand and relate to when we show it visually in pictures and videos - employer branding. We need this to be able to both attract and keep partners and employees, which is crucial to our whole business. Networking events are very important in our business.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Integrity Policy_2018-10-05.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Integrity Policy_2018-10-05.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 3", "unit_index": 3, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 3}

content: yer branding. We need this to be able to both attract and keep partners and employees, which is crucial to our whole business. Networking events are very important in our business. Therefor we need to document and show that we are very active in this respect and offer a lot of networking events for our partners, that are very well attended. Furthermore, we want to be perceived as approachable and personal unlike some of our competition, and this needs to be mirrored on our website by using personal pictures of employees and partners. Purpose In brief the purpose of displaying photos of employees and partners is to attract and keep employees and business partners which is key to Tradedoubler´s success as a business. We strongly believe that pictures rather than words show in a better way what Tradedoubler is and what we do as an employer or business partner. Information given and right to decline/object At all events all attendants are informed that photos and/or videos will be taken and also why thus the purpose and the fact that they have the possibility to object to that.

content: tradedoubler.com Balance of interest The Management of Tradedoubler have made an assessment that the benefits of Tradedoubler as an employer and business partner of showing pictures of them by far exceeds the possible risks or damages to the personal integrity of the individual on those photos, and therefore has a legitimate interest to display and present such photos. Questions re this Policy? Any questions or objections to this Policy or to any photos displayed on our website or in presentation materials may be reported to marketing@tradedoubler.com or privacy@tradedoubler.com. Photos will be removed from website and presentation materials and deleted without undue delay upon request to do so. Non-compliance with this Policy should be reported to privacy@tradedoubler.com.

content: 25.06.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 2 tradedoubler .com 1 Introduction This standard form the basis for the incident management process at T radedoubler . It describes how IT incidents are handled within T radedoubler and details the workflow for handling incidents. The standard enables T radedoubler to discover and assess incidents, to activate an organization managing the incident, and by appropriate actions restore T radedoubler’s operations to an acceptable risk level, all in an efficient manner . 1. 1 Definitions In this standard, IT incidents are defined as any incident occurring in T radedoubler’s production environment that is negatively affecting T radedoubler’s operations, including IT incidents related to T radedoubler´s cloud providers and Billing system. This includes security incidents. There are three severity levels of incidents: • Minor incident: System or service that emits warnings but does not cause a loss of service. • Moderate incident: System or service that emits warnings or errors and may cause disruption to services for some users.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "IT Incident Management Standard 2025-06-24.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\IT Incident Management Standard 2025-06-24.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 2", "unit_index": 2, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 2}

content: that emits warnings but does not cause a loss of service. • Moderate incident: System or service that emits warnings or errors and may cause disruption to services for some users. • Major incident: System or service that emits errors and causes disruption to all or a significant number of users. Incidents related to Oracle Cloud are not in scope for this standard. Incidents related to Oracle Cloud are handled by the third-party vendor Cegal according to their playbook. For incidents related to Metapic contact Erik Axelsson. Email address: erik@metapic.com 1.2 Objectives This standard provides guidelines on how to manage IT incidents within T radedoubler . It specifies roles and crucial steps for a secure and efficient incident management process. The following objectives should be adhered to: • Secure T radedoubler’s operations. • Minimizing impact on T radedoubler . • T raceability of steps and decisions taken during the process to enable improvements. 1.3 Summary The workflow outlined for incident management process at T radedoubler consist of five phases: 1.

Handling of incident: The incident is handled by the incident management team. 4. T ermination of incident management process: The incident is resolved.

content: 25.06.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 4 tradedoubler .com 2 Discovery 2. 1 Discovery of Production Environment Incident 2. 1. 1 An incident related to T radedoubler’s production environment is discovered when the PagerDuty system is generating an alert to the on-call team. The on-call team is available 24/7 /365. 2. 1.2 The on-call team performs a pre-assessment of the incident to assess whether the incident can be resolved directly, or if it needs to be escalated. The pre-assessment consider what systems are affected, how they are affected, and why they are affected. This result in an assessment of the severity level of the incident (minor , moderate, or major incident), and whether the incident needs to be escalated. 2. 1.3 If the incident can be resolved directly, no escalation is needed, and the on-call team resolve the incident or forward it to a relevant role within T radedoubler . 2. 1.4 If the incident needs escalation, the on-call team notifies the incident manager , which can be either Head of Operations or Head of Engineering. 2.

content: e within T radedoubler . 2. 1.4 If the incident needs escalation, the on-call team notifies the incident manager , which can be either Head of Operations or Head of Engineering. 2. 1.5 Incidents related to Oracle Cloud are the responsibility of by the 3rd party vendor Cegal and are primarily handled by Cegal consultants. Where there is an overlap in the affected service that can be handled by the T radedoubler on-call team, Cegal notifies T radedoubler as appropriate. 2.2 Discovery of Windows Infrastructure Incidents 2.2. 1 An incident related to the Windows infrastructure is discovered when users create tickets in the Support Portal or when an automatic alert is received by one of the monitoring systems to the system administrator . 2.2.2 The support engineer at the Support Portal or the system administrator performs a pre-assessment of the incident to assess whether the incident can be resolved directly, or if it needs to be escalated. The pre-assessment consider what systems are affected, how they are affected, and why they are affected.

content: 25.06.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 6 tradedoubler .com 3 Assessment of the Incident and Initial Communication 3. 1 The incident is assessed in terms of: • Its extent, • Affected products and services, • What resources are needed to solve the problem, • Who needs to be notified, • Legal and regulatory requirements. 3.2 If the incident is assessed as a major incident with major effect on T radedoubler’s operations and business, the board is informed. For example, when there is a total loss of service. Escalation level 2 shall be used in these cases. 3.3 The T op T ech team is responsible for communication to customers when necessary, regarding the production environment. 3.4 The Corporate IT team is responsible for communication to all internal users when necessary, regarding the Windows infrastructure. 3.5 The assessed escalation level depends on the impact and urgency of the incident, as well as the time taken to resolve it. A minor incident may be escalated to level 2 if it is not resolved within 4 hours, while a major incident may be escalated to level 2 immediately.

content: 25.06.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 7 tradedoubler .com 4 Handling of Incident 4.1 Incidents are prioritized over other current operations at T radedoubler . Work to solve an incident shall be initiated within one hour from the discovery of the incident. Minor incidents not expecting to get worse are handled based on the nature of the incident. If it is not affecting a lot of users, the incident is handled outside of the incident management process. 4.2 For incidents related to the production environment, an organisation consisting of at least the Incident Manager and one member from the on-call team are set up to manage the incident. The on-call team are operating as an incident management team upon incidents. 4.3 For incidents related to Windows infrastructure, an organisation consisting of at least the Incident Manager and Corporate IT, are set up to manage the incident. 4.4 For incidents related to the production environment, the role as Incident Manager is generally taken by the Head of Operations, or , if unavailable, Head of Engineering.

content: ident. 4.4 For incidents related to the production environment, the role as Incident Manager is generally taken by the Head of Operations, or , if unavailable, Head of Engineering. The Incident Manager is responsible for leading all work related to the incident. 4.5 For incidents related to Windows infrastructure, the role as Incident Manager is generally taken by the Head of Corporate IT. The Incident Manager is responsible for leading all work related to the incident. 4.6 The Incident Manager have the mandate to decide upon and execute appropriate actions to manage the incident, such as overtime, support and investments. 4.7 The Incident Manager is responsible for running records of actions and decisions and keep other documentation. These records should be kept in Microsoft T eams, and as a Jira ticket for incidents related to the production environment, and as Corporate IT tickets for incidents related to Windows infrastructure.

content: 25.06.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 8 tradedoubler .com 5 T ermination of Incident Management Process 5.1 The incident is terminated by the Incident Manager when all problems have been solved, and no residual problems remains if it is a major incident. For minor or moderate incidents, the on-call team terminates the incident. 5.2 Upon termination of the incident, an incident report shall be written. The incident report shall outline the incident’s background, and measures taken to handle the incident, including time stamps and responsible persons. 5.3 Incident reports of major severe incidents shall be sent to T radedoubler’s board. 5.4 The on-call team shall have a weekly meeting where incidents from the last week are discussed, including what caused the incident, how it was resolved, and how it can be avoided in the future.

content: 25.06.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 9 tradedoubler .com 6 Communication and Documentation 6. 1 Communication 6.1 .1 Internal communication regarding the incident is sent out where an incident is major severity, or moderate severity which also affects customers. Minor incidents are typically not communicated internally outside the on-call team or Corporate IT team. Internal communication is sent out after resolution of a major incident and moderate incidents (where customers are affected). Type of incident Initial message Updates Final message Major incident As soon as discovered Every hour Upon termination Moderate incident If customers are affected, notifies internally If customers are affected, as necessary If customers are affected, upon termination Minor incident None None None 6.1.2 In case of major incident, affecting a major part of T radedoubler’s services and products, T radedoubler’s board shall be notified. 6.1.3 If the incident is classified as a personal data breach, the DPO shall be informed.

content: or part of T radedoubler’s services and products, T radedoubler’s board shall be notified. 6.1.3 If the incident is classified as a personal data breach, the DPO shall be informed. 6.1.4 If personal data breach, the DPO must report the incident within 72 hours to the competent data protection authority within the country according to GDPR article 33. Data subjects shall be informed without undue delay according to GDPR article 34, if the personal data breach result in a high risk to rights and freedoms of natural persons. 6.2 Documentation 6.2.1 Records of activities and decisions taken during handling of an incident shall be written as a Jira ticket or as a Corporate IT ticket depending on the systems and applications affected. 6.2.2 The incident reports shall be documented and saved.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 2 tradedoubler.com 1 Purpose and goal T he purpose of T radedoubler ’s IT User Guidelines is to ensure the proper use of Tradedoubler’s IT systems and make users aware of what Tradedoubler deems to be acceptable and unacceptable usage. Tradedoubler reserves the right to amend this document at its discretion. In case of ame ndments, users will be informed appropriately. The goal is also that all Tradedoubler’s personnel should understand the importance of handling all data in a secure and responsible manner. Third parties should be able to trust Tradedoubler to handle sensitive information such as sales data without that information leaking to competitors or other unauthorized persons . 1.1 Motive T radedoubler’s personnel handle confidential information such as business agreements, social security numbers, bank account numbers, sales data, personal data and additional sensitive information for Tradedoubler and third parties.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 3 tradedoubler.com 2 Legal issues and requirements Email is a business tool and users are obliged to use this tool in a responsible manner. Although email is more casual than written communication, the same laws apply. By following and understanding this document the user can minimise the risks for the use r and Tradedoubler while using email. If the user disregards the rules set forth in this document, the user will be fully liable for the content of the email/emails in question. The following rules are required by law and should be strictly followed. You should be held liable if you: • Send or forward emails containing any libellous, offensive, prejudice or defamatory remarks. • Send an attachment that contains malicious code/software. • Send unsolicited email messages. • Forge or attempt to forge email messages. • Send email from another user’s email account without prior consent. • Disguise or try to disguise the user identify while sending email. • Unlawfully forward confidential information.

If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any dissemination of this communication is strictly prohibited and may be unlawful. If you have received this email in error, please notify Tradedoubler immediately and erase all copies of the message.” 2.2 Information classification All information within Tradedoubler must be handled according to the Information Classification Guidelines. It is the responsibility of each user to adhere to these.

content: 13.03.2026 Stockholm, Sweden 4 tradedoubler.com 3 Basic guidelines for handling IT resources 3.1 Private usage A lthough Tradedoubler’s IT systems is meant for business usage, Tradedoubler allows personal usage if it is reasonable and does not interfere with work or Tradedoubler’s business. It is not reasonable to play computer games, etc., during business hours and when company activities and overtime work is o ngoing in Tradedoubler offices. Private email accounts are not allowed to be downloaded to a client on the user’s computer and should preferably only be checked via web mail. No attachments from private email accounts are allowed to be downloaded without prior consent from CIT (Internal System). 3.2 Internet usage S ome private use of internet is accepted, if it is not interfering with your work. See the Information Security Standard section 6.4 for allowed usage of Tradedoubler’s devices and network resources. For any questions, please contact CIT .

Tradedoubler strives to keep a standard configuration of all hardware and components. The current standard configuration is set by CIT and is evaluated on regular base. Any exceptions from the standard configuration should be approved by the Head of CIT. 3.4 User’s accounts All accounts maintained on Tradedoubler ’s system are Tradedoubler’s property. content: Third country transfers For any of the purposes described herein, personal data may be transferred to our processors, business partners and other controllers outside of the EU/EEA (‘a third country’). We have strict guidelines to check that those recipients, which are handling your personal data, are doing so in a lawful and secure way in accordance with the data protection legislation. This means that we ensure that the transfer is only made to countries where the recipients have made sure that there is either an Adequacy Decision in place from the EU Commission, or that another approved transfer mechanism under the GDPR, including standard contractual clauses ((EU) 2021/914), have been put into place. For example, the UK, which we transfer data to, holds an Adequacy Decision. Should any of the recipients reside in a country without adequate legal protection for personal data, we will make sure the recipient adopts appropriate safeguards and enter contractual arrangements with the recipient of the personal data.

How Metapic protects your personal data: We are dedicated to maintaining the security of personal data and preventing unauthorized access or loss of data through the implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures, as far as it is reasonably possible. Such security measures also include that personal data are accessed only by a limited number of persons within Metapic. Our employees must comply with our security standard and they are all bound by confidentiality clauses. GDPR and information security staff trainings are performed annually. When we occasionally use third-party contractors to perform tasks that might otherwise be performed by our employees, these contractors are contractually bound to similar restrictions as our employees.

content: 02.05.2025 Stockholm, Sweden 13 tradedoubler .com different types of cookies to collect information about you, your electronic device (mobile phone, computer , tablet etc.) and your behaviour (e.g. how you browse a website). Cookies can either be a session cookie or a permanent cookie. A permanent cookie remains on the visitor’s computer for a set period of time. A session cookie is temporarily stored while the visitor is on a web page. Session cookies disappear when the visitor closes their browser . Cookies are used for different purposes, such as to improve the website with better experience and search possibilities on a website. Cookies can also be collected for statistical and analytical purposes. The different purposes are usually described as “categories” . We use four different categories of cookies: • Necessary cookies: Are types of cookies that are essential for the website to function properly, without which the site would not work as intended. Necessary cookies are also cookies that save certain settings you do, e.g. change language at the website.
metadata_json: {"source_file": "Privacy Policy 20250428_3.0.pdf", "source_path": "C:\\Users\\harcol\\Embeddings\\tmp\\bulk_ingest\\zip0\\GDPR related Policies\\Privacy Policy 20250428_3.0.pdf", "source_type": "pdf", "unit_label": "Page 13", "unit_index": 13, "chunk_index": 1, "chunks_in_unit": 3, "page": 13}

content: to function properly, without which the site would not work as intended. Necessary cookies are also cookies that save certain settings you do, e.g. change language at the website. Necessary cookies do not require consent and will always be placed, unless you disable cookies in your browser . • Functional cookies (“preferences”): Are types of cookies that are used to enhance the performance of a website. Without functional cookies, some features or functions of a website may not be available. • Analytics cookies: Are types of cookies used to track website visitors and their user behaviour . The collected information is used to improve the performance of the website and improve user experience. • Marketing cookies: Marketing cookies are used to track visitors across websites to allow publishers to display relevant and engaging advertisements. If you are a registered Brand or Publisher , the cookie will also manage and control your login and session on those part of our websites that are only available to registered Brands or Publishers.

content: Tradedoubler Standard tracking and GDPR/ePrivacy Tradedoubler data flows – Tradedoubler Standard tracking – Consent accepted 1. User clicks on advertiser banner/link on the publisher website. These ads and click links are obtained from the Tradedoubler Publisher Interface. 2. After a valid click a cookie is generated on the user’s device that contains a tracking identifier. The IP numbers is also collected and sent to Tradedoubler. These identifiers are used to understand which publishers were responsible for the traffic that led to a conversion. Tradedoubler also uses IP-numbers mainly for fraud and security reasons, such as identifying Bot Traffic, Cookie Stuffing and Preventing D- Dos attacks. 3. The user is redirected to the Advertiser website. When a sale/lead is made, the order data is sent to Tradedoubler servers via a tracking script. These scripts are provided by Tradedoubler during implementation. The order number, cookie identifier and IP numbers are also collected in this process. 4.

content: Anhang 2 VERTRAG ÜBER DIE VERARBEITUNG PERSONENBEZOGENER DATEN Dieser Vertrag über die Verarbeitung personenbezogener Daten („Datenverarbeitungsvertrag“) ist als wesentlicher Bestandteil des Dienstleistungsauftrags zu verstehen. Für die Zwecke dieses Datenverarbeitungsvertrags bezeichnet der Begriff „für die Verarbeitung Verantwortlicher“ den Advertiser, und der Begriff „Auftragsverarbeiter“ bezeichnet Tradedoubler. 1. Abtretung und Zweck dieses Datenverarbeitungsvertrags Die Erbringung der Dienstleistungen (gemäß Erläuterung im Dienstleistungsauftrag) umfasst die Verarbeitung personenbezogener Daten für den für die Verarbeitung Verantwortlichen. Dieser Datenverarbeitungsvertrag dient der Festlegung der Rechte und Pflichten der Parteien im Zusammenhang mit der Verarbeitung bestimmter personenbezogener Daten und anderer Informationen im Rahmen des Dienstleistungsauftrags und dieses Datenverarbeitungsvertrags.

content: teien im Zusammenhang mit der Verarbeitung bestimmter personenbezogener Daten und anderer Informationen im Rahmen des Dienstleistungsauftrags und dieses Datenverarbeitungsvertrags. Die Verarbeitung der personenbezogenen Daten erfolgt in Übereinstimmung mit geltendem Datenschutzrecht, einschließlich der Datenschutz-Grundverordnung der EU („DSGVO“) und aller Gesetze, die diese zu einem späteren Zeitpunkt ersetzen oder ergänzen. Der Auftragsverarbeiter wird in seiner Eigenschaft als Auftragsverarbeiter in Übereinstimmung mit diesem Datenverarbeitungsvertrag während dessen Laufzeit personenbezogene Daten für den für die Verarbeitung Verantwortlichen verarbeiten. 2. Zweck und Umfang der Verarbeitung personenbezogener Daten Die Verarbeitung personenbezogener Daten dient dazu, dem für die Verarbeitung Verantwortlichen zu ermöglichen, über das Publisher-Netzwerk des Auftragsverarbeiters zu werben. 3.

content: Appendix 2 PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING AGREEMENT This personal data processing agreement (the “DPA”) should be construed as an integral part of the Service Order. For the purpose of this DPA, “Data Controller” shall mean Advertiser, and “Data Processor” shall mean Tradedoubler. 1. Assignment and the purpose of this DPA The provisioning of the Services (as set out in the Service Order) will include processing personal data on behalf of Data Controller. The purpose of this DPA is to agree of the rights and obligations of the Parties as regards to the processing of certain personal data and other information under the Service Order and this DPA. The personal data shall be processed in accordance with the applicable data protection law, including the EU General Data Protection Regulation (“GDPR”) and any subsequent legislation replacing or supplementing the above. Data Processor shall, in its capacity as Data Processor, process personal data information on behalf of the Data Controller in accordance with this DPA and during the term of this DPA. 2.

content: ocessor shall, in its capacity as Data Processor, process personal data information on behalf of the Data Controller in accordance with this DPA and during the term of this DPA. 2. The purpose and scope of the personal data processing Affiliate Marketing Network: The purpose of the processing of personal data is to enable Data Controller to advertise through Data Processor’s Publishers network. White-label partner management solution: The purpose of the processing of personal data is to enable the Data Controller to manage its own publisher network using the Processor’s tracking technology including payment service as an option. 3. Categories of data subjects registered The following categories of data subjects may be included in relation to the processing under this DPA; - individuals who actively request redirection to Advertiser 4. Categories of personal data The following categories of personal data may be processed under this DPA; - cookie ID - IP number - order number - Advertiser´s contact details such as name, email address, phone no 5.

content: ollowing categories of personal data may be processed under this DPA; - cookie ID - IP number - order number - Advertiser´s contact details such as name, email address, phone no 5. Obligations of Data Controller General Data Controller shall establish relevant procedures for its own business in, e.g. in order to: - validate that there exist legal grounds according to the data protection legislation to process personal data in accordance with the purpose stated in Section 2 above; - defend the data subjects’ right to information and transparency, and the right to deletion of data etc.; - report personal data incidents to the relevant supervisory authority; and - make sure that every person who works under Data Controller’s supervision, and who will receive access to the personal data, only processes the data in accordance with specified instructions, unless otherwise is stipulated according to imperative law.

Information to Data Processor Data Controller shall immediately and in writing notify Data Processor of any and all circumstances that may arise which may involve the need to change the way in which Data Processor processes personal data. 6. Obligations of Data Processor Security Measures Data Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure that personal data is processed in accordance with the requirements in the applicable data protection law, the conditions in the Service Order and this DPA. All security measures shall be at least equal to the level which the competent supervisory authority typically requires for equivalent processing activities. The measures shall be documented and submitted to Data Controller upon written request, without undue delay.

Instructions Data Processor shall only process personal data on behalf of, and for the benefit of, the Data Controller. Furthermore, the process shall be for the purposes stated in Section 2 above and in accordance with the instructions provided by the Data Controller, and only in order to fulfil Data Processor’s its assignment in accordance with the Service Order. Data Processor shall ensure that every person who has access to the personal data covered by this DPA complies with the terms and conditions of this DPA, including the obligation to only process the personal data in accordance with the instructions given by the Data Controller, unless otherwise is stipulated according to imperative law. If Data Processor believes that the instructions given by Data Controller are in conflict with the applicable data protection legislation, Data Processor shall promptly inform Data Controller thereof.

Incident Reporting Data Processor shall promptly notify Data Controller of any security incidents where such incidents have resulted in accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of or access to the personal data covered by this DPA. All such incidents shall be documented by Data Processor and the documentation shall be delivered to Data Controller at its written request and without any delay. If it is likely that a personal data security incident involves any risk related to the privacy of data subjects, Data Processor shall immediately after when Data Processor has gained knowledge of the security incident, take sufficient remedial actions in order to prevent or mitigate the security incident’s possible negative effects.

content: Apéndice 2 ACUERDO DE TRATAMIENTO DE DATOS PERSONALES Este Acuerdo de tratamiento de datos personales (APD) debe interpretarse como una parte integral de la Orden de Servicio. A los efectos de este APD “Responsable de Tratamiento” será el Cliente y “Encargado de Tratamiento” será Tradedoubler. 1. Objeto y propósito de este APD. La provisión de los Servicios (tal y como se establece en la Orden de Servicio) debe incluir el tratamiento de datos personales en nombre del Responsable del Tratamiento. El objetivo de esta APD es establecer los derechos y obligaciones de las partes con respecto al tratamiento de ciertos datos personales y otra información bajo la Orden de Servicio y esta APD. Los datos personales se tratarán de acuerdo con la ley de protección de datos aplicable, incluyendo el Reglamento General de Protección de datos (RGPD) y cualquier otra legislación posterior que reemplace o complemente la anterior.

content: ley de protección de datos aplicable, incluyendo el Reglamento General de Protección de datos (RGPD) y cualquier otra legislación posterior que reemplace o complemente la anterior. El propósito y el alcance del tratamiento de datos personales El objetivo del tratamiento de datos personales es permitir que el Responsable del Tratamiento se anuncie a través de la red de Afiliados del Encargado de Tratamiento. 3. Categorías de Sujetos registrados Las siguientes categorías de sujetos de datos pueden incluirse en relación con el tratamiento según esta APD. - Personas que solicitan activamente la redirección al anunciante. 4. Obligaciones del Encargado del tratamiento Medidas de seguridad El Encargado de Tratamiento debe implementar medidas técnicas y organizativas apropiadas para garantizar que los datos personales son procesados de acuerdo con los requerimientos de la ley aplicable en materia de protección de datos, las condiciones de la Orden de Servicio y este APD. Todas las medidas de seguridad deben ser al menos equivalentes al nivel que la autoridad competente normalmente requiere para equivalentes actividades de tratamiento.

content: tradedoubler.com Annexe 2 - ACCORD DE TRAITEMENT DES DONNÉES PERSONNELLES Ce contrat de traitement des données personnelles (le «DPA») doit être interprété comme faisant partie intégrante du Bon de Commande. Dans le cadre de cette DPA, le terme « Data Controller » désigne l'Annonceur et le terme « Data Processor » désigne Tradedoubler. 1. AFFECTATION ET OBJECTIFS DE CE DPA Le provisionnement des Services (tel qu'indiqué dans le Bon de Commande) inclura le traitement des données personnelles pour le compte du Data Controller. Le but de ce DPA est de convenir des droits et obligations des parties en ce qui concerne le traitement de certaines données personnelles et d'autres informations en vertu de la commande de service et de cette DPA. Les données personnelles seront traitées conformément à la loi sur la protection des données applicable, y compris le règlement général de protection des données de l'UE («RGPD») et toute législation ultérieure remplaçant ou complétant ce qui précède.

L'OBJET ET LA PORTEE DU TRAITEMENT DES DONNEES PERSONNELLES Affiliate Marketing Network : Le but du traitement des données personnelles est de permettre au Data Controller de faire de la publicité via le réseau des éditeurs du Data Processor. White-label partner management solution: Le but du traitement des données personnelles est de permettre au Data Controller de gérer son propre réseau d'éditeurs en utilisant la technologie de suivi du Data Processor, y compris le service de paiement en option.
